title: succisa pratensis var. ovalis
intro: Succisa pratensis var. ovalis, also known as marsh bedstraw or oval-leaved devil's-bit, is a perennial plant species within the honeysuckle family (Caprifoliaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to stimulate blood flow, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The health benefits of succisa pratensis var. ovalis include its potential to treat conditions such as varicose veins, hemorrhoids, and ulcers, as well as its use as a diuretic and expectorant. The herbal preparations of succisa pratensis var. ovalis can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used to promote skin and tissue healing.
